commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A maintainer should decide whether these helpers are permanent plugin SDK and security-boundary contracts; automation can refresh a hash, but it cannot approve the API direction.; Needs attention: The diff makes host env override sanitizer behavior part of the public plugin SDK, so SDK/security owner review is needed before merge.; Review finding: [P2] Update the SDK API baseline hash — src/plugin-sdk/skills-... (sha=a0c595cd17b59168e82618da437a4a52d2fcfbef)
commands.review_summary:
The PR re-exports workspace skill loaders, SKILL.md frontmatter helpers, skill metadata types, and host env override sanitizer helpers from existing plugin SDK subpaths, with focused SDK tests.

PR surface: Source +20, Tests +59. Total +79 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an additive public SDK surface PR, not a bug with a failing current-main reproduction path. Source inspection confirms current main lacks the requested public exports.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer should decide whether these helpers are permanent plugin SDK and security-boundary contracts; automation can refresh a hash, but it cannot approve the API direction.

target.title: fix(plugin-sdk): expose skills helper surfaces
target.body:
Fixes #81913

## Summary
- re-export workspace skill loaders and frontmatter metadata helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/skills-runtime`
- re-export host env override sanitizer helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/sandbox`
- add focused SDK contract tests for the new public helper surface

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: third-party plugins can import OpenClaw's supported skills registry, SKILL.md frontmatter, and system-run env sanitizer helpers through public plugin SDK subpaths instead of deep-importing internals or reimplementing them.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw package build from PR head `76d0d5f1c1` on macOS. I built the dist entrypoints, generated plugin SDK declarations, ran runtime postbuild shims, then imported the package through the public self-reference specifiers `openclaw/plugin-sdk/skills-runtime` and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/sandbox`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true
node --experimental-strip-types scripts/write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts.ts
node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs
node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs
node --input-type=module -e 'const skills = await import("openclaw/plugin-sdk/skills-runtime"); const sandbox = await import("openclaw/plugin-sdk/sandbox"); const frontmatter = skills.parseFrontmatter("---\nname: Demo\nmetadata: '\''{\"openclaw\":{\"skillKey\":\"demo-key\"}}'\''\n---\n"); console.log(JSON.stringify({ skillsRuntime: { hasLoadWorkspaceSkillEntries: typeof skills.loadWorkspaceSkillEntries === "function", hasParseFrontmatter: typeof skills.parseFrontmatter === "function", skillKey: skills.resolveOpenClawMetadata(frontmatter)?.skillKey }, sandbox: { dangerousHome: sandbox.isDangerousHostEnvOverrideVarName("HOME"), sanitized: sandbox.sanitizeSystemRunEnvOverrides({ shellWrapper: true, overrides: { LANG: "en_US.UTF-8", PATH: "/tmp/bin", TERM: "xterm-256color" } }) } }, null, 2));'
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
OK: All 4 required plugin-sdk exports verified.
```

```json
{
  "skillsRuntime": {
    "hasLoadWorkspaceSkillEntries": true,
    "hasParseFrontmatter": true,
    "skillKey": "demo-key"
  },
  "sandbox": {
    "dangerousHome": true,
    "sanitized": {
      "LANG": "en_US.UTF-8",
      "TERM": "xterm-256color"
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: the built package self-reference imports resolve from the public SDK subpaths, the new skills helpers are callable, the frontmatter metadata resolver returns the expected `skillKey`, and the sandbox env sanitizer filters `PATH` while preserving allowed shell-wrapper variables.

What was not tested: I did not install the package into a separate third-party plugin repo; the proof uses the built local package self-reference and the plugin SDK export checker.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/skills-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/skills-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts`
- `node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true`
- `node --experimental-strip-types scripts/write-plugin-sdk-entry-dts.ts`
- `node scripts/runtime-postbuild.mjs`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-sdk/skills-runtime.ts src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.ts src/plugin-sdk/skills-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/sandbox.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix(compaction): add circuit breaker to stop token burn when summarizer unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

When the summarizer model is persistently unavailable (rate-limited, overloaded, misconfigured), `summarizeInStages` calls `summarizeWithFallback` for every split, each burning 3+ retries per call. With N splits, this burns N x 3 x chunkTokens before finally throwing.

Add a circuit breaker in the `summarizeInStages` split loop:

- Track consecutive generic fallback results via typed `FallbackSummaryResult.isGenericFallback`
- After 2 consecutive fallback splits, stop the loop
- Return partial summaries collected so far
- Reset counter on successful split response

Also fix TS errors and mock issues in test files:
- `compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: testModel type bypass
- `dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #58838
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

Token burn during persistent summarizer outages causes wasted API calls and slow failure recovery. Circuit breaker stops repeated fallback attempts after 2 consecutive generic failures, preserving partial summaries with useful context.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Circuit breaker prevents token burn when summarizer is persistently unavailable. After 2 consecutive generic fallback splits, the loop stops. Partial summaries that contain successful model output are not counted as generic fallbacks.

Real environment tested: Vitest test run on Linux Node 22 (local checkout).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts

✓ compaction circuit breaker > opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
✓ compaction circuit breaker > skips remaining splits and merge retry  
✓ compaction circuit breaker > happy path completes all splits

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       3 passed (3)
```

Fix applied: partialSummaryFallback now returns isGenericFallback=false (contains useful progress from earlier chunks), only truly generic fallback (no partial summary) returns isGenericFallback=true.

Observed result after fix: Partial summaries preserve useful session context while still stopping repeated calls on persistent generic failures.

What was not tested: Live compaction with real unavailable summarizer model. Unable to provide real unavailable-summarizer proof due to local environment complexity. Requesting maintainer proof override.

## Changes

- `src/agents/compaction.ts`: FallbackSummaryResult type, circuit breaker in summarizeInStages, partialSummaryFallback classification fix
- `src/agents/compaction.circuit-breaker.test.ts`: 3 regression tests + testModel type fix
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts`: getSession mock

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — design issue, not runtime bug.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Unit tests cover:
1. Breaker opens after 2 consecutive generic fallbacks
2. Remaining splits and merge retry are skipped (call-count proof)
3. Happy path completes all splits without breaker

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Sessions may retain less compacted context during sustained summarizer outages (breaker stops after 2 generic failures). Partial summaries from successful splits are preserved.

## Security Impact (required)

- [ ] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [ ] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [ ] New/changed network calls? No
- [ ] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [ ] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Circuit breaker behavior (unit tests)
- Edge cases checked: partialSummaryFallback classification (contains earlier successful chunks)
- What you did NOT verify: Live unavailable-summarizer compaction run

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

Session context may be truncated during sustained summarizer outages. Circuit breaker threshold (2) chosen to balance token savings vs context retention.

Fixes #58838
target.author: tanshanshan
